target.title: fix(gateway): accept --port on gateway health and probe (#79100)
target.body:
## Summary

`gateway run --port 18789` accepts `--port`, but the adjacent query subcommands rejected the same flag:

- `openclaw gateway health --port 18789` -> `error: unknown option '--port'`
- `openclaw gateway probe --port 18789` -> `error: unknown option '--port'`

So scripts that start a gateway on a controlled local port could not target it through `health`/`probe` with the same visible flag shape as `run`, and the `--url` workaround disables config credential fallback (forcing explicit `--token`/`--password`).

### Fix

Accept `--port` on the gateway query commands in both spellings: before the subcommand (`gateway --port 18789 health`, inherited from the parent) and after it (`gateway health --port 18789`, the exact spelling the issue reports). The port is patched onto `config.gateway.port` rather than turned into a synthetic `ws://` URL, so configured local auth and TLS scheme selection are preserved.

- `gatewayCallOpts` accepts `--port`, so `health`, `call`, `usage-cost`, and `stability` accept the flag after the subcommand.
- `resolveGatewayRpcOptions` still inherits the parent `--port` for the before-subcommand spelling.
- `callGatewayCli` loads the real config and overrides only `gateway.port`.
- `gateway probe` passes a numeric `port` to `gatewayStatusCommand`, which patches `config.gateway.port` before `resolveTargets` derives the scheme.

Fixes #79100

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: `openclaw gateway health --port <N>` and `openclaw gateway probe --port <N>` should accept the subcommand-position port flag and connect to a real gateway on that non-default local port by patching `config.gateway.port`, while preserving configured auth/TLS instead of requiring a synthetic `--url`.

**Real environment tested**: Local Linux source checkout on commit `39fc9902f1`, using an isolated temporary OpenClaw home/config under `/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/.proof-89623/live-port-19323`, a real `openclaw gateway run` process listening on loopback port `19323`, and the built `node openclaw.mjs` CLI entry for the client commands. The temporary config was `gateway.mode=local`, `gateway.bind=loopback`, and `gateway.auth.mode=none`; `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`/`OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1` only skipped external channel sidecars.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```sh
PROOF_ROOT=/media/vdc/0668001470/workSpace/claw-campaign/.proof-89623/live-port-19323
export HOME="$PROOF_ROOT/home"
export OPENCLAW_HOME="$PROOF_ROOT/home"
export OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$PROOF_ROOT/home/.openclaw"
export OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/openclaw.json"
export OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1
export OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDERS=1
export OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1

node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --dev --reset --port 19323 --bind loopback --auth none --allow-unconfigured --ws-log compact
# After the gateway logged `http server listening` and `ready`, set gateway.auth.mode=none in the isolated config so the CLI preserves matching local auth config.
node openclaw.mjs gateway health --port 19323 --json --timeout 15000
node openclaw.mjs gateway probe --port 19323 --json --timeout 8000
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Gateway startup reached the real listener on port `19323`:

```text
[gateway] starting HTTP server...
[gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 1.7s)
[gateway] ready
```

The subcommand-position health command returned a real gateway health payload:

```text
$ node openclaw.mjs gateway health --port 19323 --json --timeout 15000 | node -e ...
{
  "ok": true,
  "defaultAgentId": "dev",
  "agents": [
    {
      "agentId": "dev",
      "isDefault": true
    }
  ]
}
```

The subcommand-position probe command reached the same non-default local port and completed the read probe:

```text
$ node openclaw.mjs gateway probe --port 19323 --json --timeout 8000 | node -e ...
{
  "ok": true,
  "degraded": false,
  "primaryTargetId": "localLoopback",
  "localLoopbackUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:19323",
  "target": {
    "id": "localLoopback",
    "url": "ws://127.0.0.1:19323",
    "connectOk": true,
    "rpcOk": true,
    "healthOk": true,
    "configValid": true,
    "authMode": "none"
  }
}
```

The focused Commander/RPC option tests also pass:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli.coverage.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  23 passed (23)
```

**Observed result after fix**: Both `gateway health --port 19323` and `gateway probe --port 19323` accepted the port after the subcommand and successfully connected to a real gateway on `ws://127.0.0.1:19323`. `probe` reported `connectOk: true`, `rpcOk: true`, `healthOk: true`, and `authMode: "none"`, proving the command used the loaded local config with only the port overridden.

**What was not tested**: TLS (`wss://`) and token/password auth variants were not live-tested in this proof; the focused tests cover that the implementation preserves config-based auth/TLS selection by patching `config.gateway.port` instead of forcing a `--url` override.
target.author: alix-007
